question_title: What will be the value of "ICE BofA High Yield Emerging Markets Corporate Plus Index Option-Adjusted Spread" on 2026-04-08?
summary: The ICE BofA High Yield EM Corporate Plus Index OAS has been widening sharply, from 3.26 in late February to 3.94 on March 30, 2026. The widening is driven by trade policy uncertainty: Section 122 tariffs (10%) imposed after the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs, new Section 301 investigations launched March 11, and general market stress (S&P 500 down ~7% from highs). The April 2, 2026 anniversary of Liberation Day generates media coverage but is not a specific policy catalyst like the original April 2, 2025 announcement. My combined forecast (50% scenario Monte Carlo + 30% empirical bootstrap from widening regimes + 20% trend extrapolation) centers around 3.98 with a right-skewed distribution reflecting upside tail risk from potential tariff escalation (Trump's stated intent to raise Section 122 to 15%). Empirical mean-reversion tendency after widening episodes moderates the forecast center somewhat below pure trend extrapolation.

factors.description: Strong recent widening trend: spread rose from 3.26 (late Feb) to 3.94 (Mar 30), accelerating in last week (+0.27 in 4 days). Active policy drivers (Section 301 investigations, Section 122 tariffs) support continued pressure.

No specific new tariff shock event on April 2, 2026 (unlike April 2, 2025 Liberation Day announcement). The anniversary generates news coverage but isn't a policy catalyst. Section 301 investigations take months to produce tariffs.
Empirical mean-reversion tendency: historical 7-day changes following widening regimes show mean reversion (mean change -0.07, median -0.12). After rapid widening, some stabilization is typical.
S&P 500 down ~7% from 52-week high, credit markets under stress. RSM increased recession probability to 20%. General risk-off environment supports wider spreads.
Potential tariff escalation risk: Trump announced intent to raise Section 122 from 10% to 15%. If implemented around anniversary week, could cause spike. April 2025 precedent: +1.06 in 8 days.
factors.conditional: If Section 122 tariff increased to 15% or other escalation occurs

factors.description: Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs (Feb 2026). Legal constraints limit Trump's tariff tools. Trade deals previously reduced rates. Structural improvement since April 2025 peak of 4.90.

reasoning: The reviewer approved my analysis. Let me now produce the final output.
sources_consulted: [Market minute: Widening credit spreads denote rising risk from trade policy](https://realeconomy.rsmus.com/market-minute-widening-credit-spreads-denote-rising-risk-from-trade-policy/)
[Credit Markets on Edge as Tariff Pressure Builds | The Daily Economy](https://thedailyeconomy.org/article/credit-markets-on-edge-as-tariff-pressure-builds/)
[Tracking the Economic Impact of the Trump Tariffs](https://taxfoundation.org/research/all/federal/trump-tariffs-trade-war/)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series BAMLEMHBHYCRPIOAS once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=BAMLEMHBHYCRPIOAS`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-04-08 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
